name: Hotel and Travel Industry
description_short: Hawaii-based federal credit union chartered in 1954, serving hotel workers and their families with member-owned financial products and services.
description_long:
Hotel & Travel Industry Federal Credit Union was chartered in 1954 as a not-for-profit, member-owned cooperative specifically designed to serve the financial needs of Hawaii state hotel workers, their families, and household members. The organization operates under the NCUA (National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund), providing members with deposit insurance up to $250,000. Based in Honolulu with a physical location at 1914 S. King Street, the credit union has established itself as a community-focused financial institution for over 70 years.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), certificates, auto loans, personal loans, lines of credit, credit cards, and home equity loans. Members benefit from online and mobile banking with unlimited free bill pay, access to a shared branching network through both Hawaii CO-OP and the National CO-OP Share Branching Network for transactions at participating credit unions nationwide, and on-site visit services arranged directly at hotel properties. Additional member benefits include bilingual transaction assistance, discounted life insurance through CUNA Mutual, preferred auto dealer pricing, and discounts with companies like Sprint, Nextel, and Dell.

What distinguishes Hotel & Travel Industry FCU is its explicit occupational focus and commitment to member relationships rather than transactional banking. The credit union conducts on-site visits at hotels to provide real-time loan applications, account openings, and payroll deduction changes directly to workers. Membership eligibility is tied to Local 5 Union membership and hotel employment in Hawaii, though members retain lifetime membership even after employment changes or retirement. This niche positioning creates a tight-knit member community reflected in their annual membership meetings with in-person and virtual options.

The primary limitation of this credit union is its geographic and occupational restriction—membership is limited to Hawaii hotel workers and their families, making it inaccessible to the general population. The website lacks detailed information on loan rates, fees, account minimums, and APRs, requiring members to contact the institution directly. As a regional credit union with a single physical location, in-person services are restricted to the Honolulu area, though the shared branching network partially mitigates this constraint.
services: Lines of credit with overdraft protection
Online and mobile banking with unlimited bill pay
Shared branching network access (30,000+ locations nationwide)
On-site workplace visits for loan applications and account services
pros: Lifetime membership once eligible—members remain members even if employment or circumstances change
Access to 30,000+ shared branch locations nationwide through Hawaii CO-OP and National CO-OP networks for deposits, withdrawals, and basic transactions
Free unlimited bill pay with online and mobile banking services
On-site services at hotel properties—loan applications, account opening, and payroll changes available directly at workplaces
Bilingual transaction assistance for non-English speaking members
NCUA insurance protection up to $250,000 per member deposit
Member discounts on life insurance, auto dealer pricing, and select retailers (Sprint, Nextel, Dell)
cons: Restricted membership—only eligible to Hawaii hotel workers (Local 5 Union members) and their families, making it inaccessible to general consumers
Limited physical locations—single Honolulu branch at 1914 S. King Street limits in-person service access for members outside the immediate area
Lack of transparent rate and fee information on website—requires direct contact to learn APRs, account minimums, and monthly fees
No information on loan approval timelines, credit requirements, or lending standards
Minimal digital presence details—website lacks comprehensive FAQs about specific products and services compared to larger financial institutions
best_for: Hotel workers in Hawaii employed by Local 5 Union member properties seeking community-focused financial services
Hawaii hospitality industry employees wanting workplace-accessible banking and on-site loan services
Hotel workers who value member-owner relationships and prefer credit union membership over traditional banks
Families of eligible hotel employees needing lifetime financial stability with membership benefits that persist after employment changes
diagnosis: Hotel & Travel Industry FCU is exclusively designed for Hawaii hotel workers affiliated with Local 5 Union and their families—a highly specialized credit union not available to the general public. The primary caveat is occupational eligibility restriction; non-hotel workers and those outside Hawaii cannot become members, making this institution inaccessible to most consumers seeking credit union services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Hotel and Travel Industry (Honolulu, HI) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Hotel and Travel Industry — NCUA-insured credit union in Honolulu, HI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
